exercises.prompt: « Les jeunes n'ont plus AUCUN repère » — what marks this as provocation?

options.text: The absolute « aucun » — polemics speak in absolutes

exercises.explanation: Tous, jamais, aucun — absolutes are the polemicist's drumbeat. Spot them, then look underneath.

exercises.prompt: To pass things down across generations: « ___ un patrimoine, des valeurs. »
exercises.explanation: Léguer = to bequeath. (Larguer = to dump — a very different family dynamic.)

exercises.template: Le fossé générationnel se {{b1}} d'année en année.
exercises.explanation: Se creuser = to widen (its opposite: se combler, to close up).

exercises.prompt: The C1 exam move on a polemic.
exercises.template: L'auteur {{b1}} pour interpeller, mais son constat de fond est mesuré.
exercises.explanation: Forcer le trait = to deliberately overstate — name the rhetoric, then extract the substance.

exercises.prompt: Reading a polemic at C1 means…

options.text: separating the provocation from the underlying argument

exercises.explanation: A polemic wants a reaction; you answer with analysis: rhetoric named, claim extracted.

exercises.prompt: To question what came before (three words: remettre en …).
exercises.template: Chaque génération remet en {{b1}} la précédente.
exercises.explanation: Remettre en cause = to call into question.

exercises.prompt: Match each generations word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "A youth in search of bearings."
Listen to the radio columnist. What is the measured claim under the heat?
exercises.transcript: On accuse les soixante-huitards d'avoir tout légué sauf des repères — derrière la provocation, le vrai constat : les rythmes de vie ont divergé.

options.text: That generations' rhythms of life have diverged
That the 68ers truly left nothing at all
That young people refuse all inheritance

exercises.explanation: The provocation is the accusation; the constat de fond is the diverging rhythms.

exercises.prompt: Read this (deliberately) polemical column and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: « Les jeunes ne lisent plus, n'écoutent plus, ne respectent plus rien ! » Vraiment ? La même phrase se lit, mot pour mot, dans des chroniques de 1968, de 1925 — et, paraît-il, sur une tablette sumérienne. La querelle des générations est moins un constat qu'un rituel : chaque génération remet en cause la précédente, hérite malgré elle de ses repères, puis s'indigne à son tour. Le déluge n'arrive jamais ; il se transmet.
exercises.passageEn: "Young people no longer read, no longer listen, no longer respect anything!" Really? The same sentence appears, word for word, in columns from 1968, from 1925 — and, reportedly, on a Sumerian tablet. The quarrel of generations is less a finding than a ritual: each generation questions the previous one, inherits its bearings despite itself, then takes its turn being indignant. The flood never comes; it gets handed down.

questions.prompt: What rhetorical device opens the column?

options.text: A provocation in absolutes (ne…plus rien), immediately questioned

questions.prompt: What is the author's constat de fond?

options.text: The quarrel of generations is a recurring ritual, not a real decline
Young people have truly stopped reading
someone from the May 1968 protest generation
a person who is sixty-eight years old

exercises.explanation: The soixante-huitards are the May 68 generation — cultural shorthand every C1 text assumes.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Le fossé générationnel se creuse moins qu'on ne le croit.

options.text: The generation gap is widening less than people think.
What will we bequeath, if not our questions?
Each generation questions the bearings of the one before.

exercises.prompt: What does « le fossé générationnel » mean?
exercises.explanation: le fossé générationnel = the generation gap.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « La querelle des générations est éternelle. » — a quarrel / feud.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “What will we bequeath, if not our questions?”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Le fossé générationnel se creuse moins qu'on ne le croit.
exercises.targetEn: The generation gap is widening less than people think.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Chaque génération remet en cause les repères de la précédente.
exercises.targetEn: Each generation questions the bearings of the one before.
